content:
# Recommendations — for iter-013 plan

## ⚠️ TOP: 4 iters, 0 sorry-elimination on BOTH routes — STOP blind re-fire
- FBC sorry 4→4→5→5→5; SNAP 5→6→6→6→7. Neither route has ELIMINATED a sorry since iter-008.
  Both pc-reversal signals tripped (iter-010, iter-011). **Do NOT re-fire a prover on either route
  with the same recipe.** Each route is now at a precisely-characterised blocker that the
  element-level / tactic-level approaches have provably exhausted. Both correctives are
  consult-first (mathlib-analogist), NOT another prover round.
- progress-critic is MANDATORY next plan phase; expect CHURNING/STUCK on both — act on it, do not
  rebut without a structural change.

## CRITICAL — both blockers need a mathlib-analogist consult BEFORE any prover
**Note on tooling:** the prover reported "no API key → could not consult". That only blocks the
external `archon-informal-agent.py`. The **`mathlib-analogist` subagent IS available** (it is a
Claude subagent, key-independent) — the planner CAN and SHOULD dispatch it. The iter-013 corrective
is unblocked.

1. **FBC `gammaPushforwardIso_comp` (FlatBaseChange.lean L733/sorry@784)** — element-wise collapse
   PROVABLY EXHAUSTED (whole-goal defeq, monolithic rfl, term-mode Eq.trans all VERIFIED cold-build
   fails; see session_12 milestones). Blocker = value-`ModuleCat`/`X.Modules` object-junction
   diamond (carrier-rfl cheap, per-junction object-defeq = whnf kernel bomb).
   - **mathlib-analogist (api-alignment or cross-domain):** how to collapse a value-`ModuleCat`
     carrier-identity composite WITHOUT forming heavy per-junction object defeqs. Concrete lead the
     prover surfaced: `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app` routes through `.val.app (op ⊤)`
     at the SHEAF level where restrictScalars/globalSections object-junctions never form. Open
     question: how to express B/A/γ_φ (restrictScalars-level) sheaf-level — likely an `Iso.ext` /
     inverse-comparison route that never element-chases, or a structural lemma identifying the whole
     `gammaPushforwardIso` object-wise.
   - Then a blueprint-writer should rewrite the `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp` proof to the
     sheaf-level route (see the `% NOTE` I added at the proof block).

2. **SNAP hK_lhs/hK_rhs (SectionGradedRing.lean L1903/L1960)** — the μ-pair `μ.hom ≫ μ.inv` does NOT
   cancel even on the ISOLATED small goal (corrects the iter-011 "isolation will fix it" premise).
   Blocker = `CategoryStruct.comp` instance mismatch: keystone inner-comp is `Localization.Monoidal`
   localized-category comp, boundary comp is `X.Modules` synonym comp (defeq, NOT syntactic).
   `Category.assoc` can't bridge; `simp [Localization.Monoidal.μ]` times out at isDefEq.
   - **mathlib-analogist (api-alignment):** the canonical instance-agnostic way to cancel
     `e.hom ≫ e.inv` across the `LocalizedMonoidal`-synonym / base-`comp` boundary (a `change` to
     `(L').obj`-level comp, or a dedicated `Localization.Monoidal` cancellation idiom). This single
     cancel unblocks BOTH halves: hK_rhs then closes with `+ μ_natural_right + counit triangle`;
     hK_lhs with `+ associator_naturality + associator_hom_app + the same cancel`.
   - Then a blueprint-writer should rewrite hK_lhs's "whnf-at-scale artifact" prose (see the `% NOTE`
     I added) — it is contradicted by the verified finding.

## Closest-to-completion
- **SNAP associator bridge** is closest: the assembly own-body is sorry-free; ONE shared idiom (the
  μ-cancel across the comp boundary) unblocks BOTH halves and cascades to the 5 gated coherences
  (`tensorPowAdd_*`, `sectionsMul_mul_assoc`) + the graded-ring instance. High-leverage single fix.
- **FBC foundation** is one (structural, not tactical) lemma from done; its closure ungates the crux
  `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural` (L1424).

## Do NOT re-assign (blocked, would repeat verified failures)
- FBC `gammaPushforwardIso_comp` via ANY element-wise collapse (rw/simp matching, term Eq.trans,
  monolithic/whole-goal rfl) — all 3 families verified-fail on cold build.
- SNAP μ-cancel via `Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc` / `Category.assoc` re-association / `simp [Localization.Monoidal.μ]`
  unfold — verified no-progress / isDefEq-timeout, INCLUDING on the isolated goal.

## MAJOR (lean-auditor iter012) — latent build-bomb to fix in a prover round
- `FlatBaseChange.lean:1488` and `:1523-1526` — `set_option maxHeartbeats` directives scope to the
  `/-! ... -/` section comments, NOT the theorems. `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (L1546) runs
  under DEFAULT budget; will SILENTLY time out when its sorry is filled. iter-011 latent bug, STILL
  UNFIXED. Fix = move the `set_option ... in` to immediately precede the theorem (a docstring is part
  of the decl, but a `/-! -/` block is a separate command). Assign in the next FBC prover round.
  (Note: these are on the COMPILE-DEAD mate decls, off the live FBC-B route — low current urgency but
  a real future trap; bundle with the mate-excision iter.)

## Coverage debt (blueprint these — when there is Lean there must be tex)
- SNAP: `assocCommonForm` (L1847) — the well-typed 5-factor `K`; chapter `K` schematic is 4-factor and
  omits the seg-1 unit prefix. Blueprint a `def:assocCommonForm` block + `\lean{}` (snap012 major #1).
- FBC: 4 new helpers need `lem:` blocks — `moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply` (L696),
  `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_concreteApply` (L706), `restrictScalars_map_concreteApply` (L713),
  `restrictScalarsComp_inv_app_concreteApply` (L720). All trivial `:= rfl`/`subst;rfl`.

## MINOR
- FBC `.lean` docstring L813 "pointwise reflexivity" is stale (next FBC prover should fix; blueprint
  L917 already correct).
- Stale iter-NNN comments (FBC L183-248; SNAP planning blocks) — readability only.
- SNAP `instGMonoid/instGSemiring/instGCommSemiring` docstrings don't flag their transitive sorry.

## Reusable patterns discovered (also in PROJECT_STATUS Knowledge Base)
- `moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply` (`subst h; rfl`): kernel-light replacement for a
  `change`/`rfl` eqToHom collapse — turns an `eqToHom` application into a SYMBOLIC carrier `cast`
  without forcing a whnf of the structure-sheaf machinery.
- SNAP prefix-strip idiom: `rw [tensorObjAssoc]; simp only [unfold]; refine congrArg (fun t => _ ≫ t) ?_`
  (×2) to peel a shared composite prefix off both sides before the hard tail.
